content: Find empirical formula of the compound if $$M=68 \%$$ (atomic mass $$=34$$ ) and remaining $$32 \%$$ oxygen.
explanation:
Given,

$$\%$$ of element $$(M)=68 \%$$

and of oxygen $$(\mathrm{O})=32 \%$$

Atomic mass of $$M=34$$

Thus, empirical formula of $$M_x \mathrm{O}_y$$ is

element $$\longrightarrow \%$$ of mass $$\longrightarrow$$ moles $$(n)$$

$$\begin{aligned} & M \longrightarrow 68 \longrightarrow 68 / 34=2 \\ & \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 32 \longrightarrow 32 / 16=2 \end{aligned}$$

Hence, empirical forumula of given compound is $$\mathrm{M}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$$ or $$M \mathrm{O}$$.

Thus, option (a) is the correct.
